fault_hypothesis: The condition `p[0] <= end` on line 75 incorrectly includes points at the exclusive upper boundary of the time range. It should be `p[0] < end` to match the documented half-open interval `[start, end)`.

constraints.intent: The returned points must have timestamps strictly less than the end boundary.

spec.expr: not any(p[0] == end for p in points)

constraints.intent: The buggy code only produces a correct result for tests where no input point lies exactly on the end boundary.

spec.expr: len(__cbfl_result) == sum(1 for p in points if start <= p[0] < end)

constraints.intent: The number of returned points must match the count of points satisfying the correct half-open interval condition.
